description: A Class D conduct artifact whose commit_chain has stage numbers or created_at timestamps that contradict each other MUST receive CONDUCT_INVALID. Commit ordering must remain externally auditable (CC4).

input.claim: the recommended strategy is to ship F-1 as a class-scoped governance correction

not_optimizing_for: preservation of existing WISEATA wire format

reasoning_trace.claim: single-rule lock at genesis is upstream

not_optimizing_for: preservation of existing WISEATA wire format

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 invariant CC4: commit ordering MUST remain externally auditable. The commit_chain above presents stage 2 first and stage 1 second, with created_at timestamps that contradict the stage numbering — stage 2 (12:00:00Z) is dated BEFORE stage 1 (12:00:01Z), yet stage 2 declares depends_on the hash of stage 1. Stage 2 cannot have committed to a stage that did not yet exist. A conformant verifier MUST reject the artifact as CONDUCT_INVALID. Auditable ordering means stage numbers monotonically increase, created_at monotonically increases, and depends_on references the literal prior stage.
